WaitingTimeVsEnergy_13_07_2026_14_02_51 — Interactive Experiment Report

Objectives: Average Waiting Time (s) vs Energy (kWh) (both minimized) · Plots rendered with matplotlib (EAF publication style, as plot_scenario_pareto.py) · generated 2026-07-13 11:23 UTC

PowerCap Views

Universal Pareto

Colour and visibility apply to every plot, legend entry and table at once. Each plot has its own title box and an axis-swap button.

Pareto Fronts

Multi-objective algorithms: 50% empirical attainment surface with [25%, 75%] band across seeds. Single-objective algorithms: median with IQR error bars. Axes globally normalized to ideal (0,0) and nadir (1,1); raw units on the secondary axes. HV in the legend is the pooled median [IQR] per algorithm across ALL (scenario, seed) runs, computed from recomputed HV_fixed (scenario-fixed reference point, from quality_indicators_all_scenarios.csv) — the same figure plot_scenario_pareto.py publishes. It intentionally differs from the per-scenario HV means shown in the Quality Indicators section below. The Points-view button on each plot switches to a plain raw-unit scatter of every algorithm’s pooled Pareto set, with the Universal Pareto set overlaid as open black rings — the coloured marker inside a ring is the algorithm that contributed that point.

X: Average Waiting Time (s) / Y: Energy (kWh)
X: Average Waiting Time (s) / Y: Energy (kWh)
X: Average Waiting Time (s) / Y: Energy (kWh)

Quality Indicators — Plots

Bars are per-algorithm MEAN values per scenario, taken verbatim from scenario_<N>_performance_metrics.csv (the Java-computed indicators — NOT the pooled HV_fixed used in the Pareto plot legends), plus the Universal_Pareto reference. GD and IGD of the universal set are 0 by definition (it is the reference front). For Pareto contribution, the CSV MEAN row holds the TOTAL number of universal-front points contributed across all seeds (the universal bar is the front size). Swapping axes turns the chart horizontal.

X: scenario / Y: HV (mean)
X: scenario / Y: HV_fixed (mean)
X: scenario / Y: GD (mean)
X: scenario / Y: IGD (mean)
X: scenario / Y: ParetoContribution (total)

Quality Indicators — Tables

Values verbatim from scenario_<N>_performance_metrics.csv (the same tables the scripts in scripts/ consume): one row per seed plus MEAN and STDDEV, and the Universal_Pareto reference row. Click a seeds button to expand.

Seed Collaboration (per-seed universal front sharing, near-tie credit)

Each seed's own all-arms universal front and each algorithm's share of it (near-tie credit at 0.3% relative tolerance, nearest-match) — distributional collaboration evidence, unlike the pooled best-of-all-seeds universal front above.
